problem_statement: Automated security testing in CI/CD pipelines produces excessive false positives and treats all LLM predictions as equally reliable, eroding developer trust; the paper addresses how to route AI-generated vulnerability assessments between automated gates and human review based on model confidence.
magnitude_evidence: Developers face floods of security alerts with weak precision; teams mute scans, defer reviews, and adopt a 'fix later' mentality; industrial reports document these frictions extensively [1],[2]
insufficiency_evidence: Bandit and Semgrep report detection rates of 62.7% and 47.6%, missing 37.3% (Bandit) and 52.4% (Semgrep) of validation vulnerabilities; and LLMs treated as single-shot classifiers report high confidence on incorrect results causing developers to over-rely on wrong predictions
solution_mechanisms: confidence-based routing between automated gates and human review
